for he felt that despite all Genji’s insistence, he ought never to

The duke was astounded, the duchess amazed, Don Quixote

pairs.genji_source:
When Higekuro
saw the basket with its accompanying note, he burst out laughing: ‘What
an extraordinary man this Genji is!’
pairs.quijote_source:
The duke was astounded, the duchess amazed, Don Quixote
wondering, Sancho Panza trembling, and indeed, even they who were aware
of the cause were frightened.

pairs.display: As for Kumoi, he remembered how often he had praised in herand indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_half: As for Kumoi, he remembered how often he had praised in her
pairs.quijote_half: and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_source:
As for Kumoi,
he remembered how often he had praised in her hearing the pleasant
relations that prevailed between the members of Genji’s household.
pairs.quijote_source:
The duke was astounded, the duchess amazed, Don Quixote
wondering, Sancho Panza trembling, and indeed, even they who were aware
of the cause were frightened.

pairs.display: Koremitsu’s son Hyōye no Jō was now sent to dig them up,and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_half: Koremitsu’s son Hyōye no Jō was now sent to dig them up,
pairs.quijote_half: and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_source:
Koremitsu’s son Hyōye no Jō
was now sent to dig them up, and they were finally laid before Prince
Sochi by Yūgiri.
pairs.quijote_source:
The duke was astounded, the duchess amazed, Don Quixote
wondering, Sancho Panza trembling, and indeed, even they who were aware
of the cause were frightened.

pairs.display: It was of course quite wrong that Kaoru, the child of a commoner,and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_half: It was of course quite wrong that Kaoru, the child of a commoner,
pairs.quijote_half: and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_source:
It was of course quite wrong that Kaoru, the child of a
commoner, should be brought up with the Akashi Princess’s children.
pairs.quijote_source:
The duke was astounded, the duchess amazed, Don Quixote
wondering, Sancho Panza trembling, and indeed, even they who were aware
of the cause were frightened.

pairs.display: I know no one else at Court to compare with her in this respect.’and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_half: I know no one else at Court to compare with her in this respect.’
pairs.quijote_half: and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_source:
I know no one else at Court to
compare with her in this respect.’

‘I have always heard,’ said Murasaki, ‘that Lady Oborozuki is
extremely accomplished and quick-witted.
pairs.quijote_source:
The duke was astounded, the duchess amazed, Don Quixote
wondering, Sancho Panza trembling, and indeed, even they who were aware
of the cause were frightened.

pairs.display: Throughout this time Genji was constantly reminded of his separation from Oborozuki at the timeand indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_half: Throughout this time Genji was constantly reminded of his separation from Oborozuki at the time
pairs.quijote_half: and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_source:
Throughout this time Genji was constantly reminded of his separation
from Oborozuki at the time when she became Consort of the Emperor
Suzaku.
pairs.quijote_source:
The duke was astounded, the duchess amazed, Don Quixote
wondering, Sancho Panza trembling, and indeed, even they who were aware
of the cause were frightened.

pairs.display: It so happened that Genji was actually at Akikonomu’s palace when the scented bales arrived;and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_half: It so happened that Genji was actually at Akikonomu’s palace when the scented bales arrived;
pairs.quijote_half: and indeed, even they who were aware of the cause were frightened.
pairs.genji_source:
It so happened that Genji was actually at Akikonomu’s palace
when the scented bales arrived; her servants at once showed them to him
and told him whence they came.
pairs.quijote_source:
The duke was astounded, the duchess amazed, Don Quixote
wondering, Sancho Panza trembling, and indeed, even they who were aware
of the cause were frightened.
